title: Special collectors edition manga
wikitext:
[]: Special collectors edition manga?             Quote Post by Kye7 » Mon Oct 31, 2011 5:00 pm Was at school when I happened to notice these on some shelves :O These are both the second books/volumes What exactly is the difference? I didn't really have time to open them up, as I was getting to class.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon Oct 31, 2011 5:13 pm The one on the left is the second edition. The one on the right is the special edition of the first edition. Way back in the day, all manga was mirrored when released in North America. As part of the agreement, in accordance with Sadamoto's wishes, a special edition was released alongside the normal version to allow readers to read it in its original orientation, with Japanese sound effects. This was the first instance of a manga being released in this fashion. They released volumes 1-7 in this format. If you're looking to collect the Evangelion manga, you're going to want to get the second edition format, which currently covers the entire 12-volume run.

[]:             Quote Post by Kye7 » Mon Oct 31, 2011 5:48 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: The one on the left is the second edition. The one on the right is the special edition of the first edition. Way back in the day, all manga was mirrored when released in North America. As part of the agreement, in accordance with Sadamoto's wishes, a special edition was released alongside the normal version to allow readers to read it in its original orientation, with Japanese sound effects. This was the first instance of a manga being released in this fashion. They released volumes 1-7 in this format. If you're looking to collect the Evangelion manga, you're going to want to get the second edition format, which currently covers the entire 12-volume run.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon Oct 31, 2011 5:54 pm Kye7 wrote: So the specail collectors edition just has all the sound effects like BOOM, CRACK, BANG, just the sound effects are in Japanese? So there's no real difference in the content, correct?
